blockSlide.calibrationSubtitle: Map the horizontal axis on screen, then aim to match it with intention alone.
blockSlide.sessionAbout: You steer a single block side to side. Imagine a gentle nudge left, right, or back to center — that is how you train a clear intention and a calm, steady focus before a longer run or a recording.
blockSlide.title: Slide the block

control.startHint: Start the drill now, or launch a BCI model in the panel first.

forwardGate.about: Gates approach from above — steer the sphere into each opening with motor imagery (left/right hand, same mapping as Neuro-Balance). You pass when the sphere crosses the gate inside the gap; green marks your alignment on the active gate only.

complete.subtitle: You passed {n} out of {total} gates ({pct}% success rate).
complete.title: Session complete

durationDesc.10: Standard training session — recommended for most users.
durationDesc.15: Advanced run — expect sustained focus for the full duration.
durationDesc.5: Short run — a good warm-up and daily habit.

config.subtitle: Choose session length before you start
config.title: Session settings

forwardGate.goToExerciseHint: You can set up the BCI model now or launch it later.

ready.subtitle: Press Start (Space). Gates scroll from above — use ← → or BCI to steer into each gap. Green shows you are aligned with the current gate.
ready.title: Ready?

forwardGate.title: Forward Gate Control

hub.cardBlockSlideDescription: That is how you train a clear intention and a calm, steady focus before a longer run or a recording.
hub.cardBlockSlideLead: You steer a single block side to side. Imagine a gentle nudge left, right, or back to center.
hub.cardForwardGateDescription: Steer through openings before they reach you — smooth horizontal control matters more than reflex.
hub.cardForwardGateLead: Gates drift toward you from above; align with each gap using motor imagery (same as Neuro-Balance).
hub.cardNeuroBalanceDescription: Mu/Beta focus training: you practice sustained attention and a stable BCI signal without game-like chrome.
hub.cardNeuroBalanceLead: Keep a levitating sphere in the center using motor imagery of your hands.
hub.subtitle: Calm, repeatable sessions: one axis, one intention at a time — you practice holding attention and replaying a movement in your mind. Step by step, training focus and a stable signal for BCI, at your own pace.
hub.title: Mind exercises

neuroBalance.about: Keep a luminous sphere near the center using motor imagery. If it drifts left, imagine your right hand. If it drifts right, imagine your left. You train sustained focus and a clear Mu/Beta control signal.

durationDesc.10: Standard training session — recommended for most users.
durationDesc.15: Advanced run — expect sustained focus for the full duration.
durationDesc.5: Short run — a good warm-up and daily habit.

config.subtitle: Choose how long the exercise runs before you start
config.title: Session settings

neuroBalance.goToExerciseHint: You can set up the BCI model now or launch it later.

hint.center: Hold the sphere in the focus band
hint.left: The sphere leans right — use left-hand imagery
hint.right: The sphere leans left — use right-hand imagery

ready.subtitle: Press Start (Space). While running, ← → match left- and right-hand BCI commands.
ready.title: Ready?

recovering.subtitle: The sphere lost balance. Take a calm breath and try again.
recovering.title: Relax

sessionComplete.subtitle: Longest uninterrupted focus in this session: {s} s.
sessionComplete.title: Session complete

neuroBalance.title: Neuro-Balance
